title: Fix the Cascading Return Type Change
description: A function's return type changed from dict to a dataclass, breaking consumers.
pr_diff:
PR Diff:
  - def fetch_config() -> dict:
  -     return {"host": "db.local", "port": 5432}
  + @dataclass
  + class Config:
  +     host: str
  +     port: int
  + def fetch_config() -> Config:
  +     return Config(host="db.local", port=5432)
initial_ci_output:
[FAILED] mypy
error: Item "Config" of ... has no attribute "__getitem__" [index-error] (x4)

[FAILED] unit_tests
TypeError: 'Config' object is not subscriptable

[FAILED] integration
KeyError: config['host'] no longer valid

files.tests/test_integration.py:
config = fetch_config()
assert config["host"] == "db.local" and config["port"] == 5432

unit_tests.failure_output: TypeError: 'Config' object is not subscriptable in test_config_host

integration.failure_output: KeyError: 'Config' object does not support [] access in integration test

full_suite.failure_output: full suite still has failures from dict→dataclass migration
